name: Phase 6 resource planning and conflict resolution requirements matrix

rows.requirement: Resource planning API and governed commands
evidence: apps/api/src/phase6Runtime.ts implements deterministic Phase 6 resource load read model, overload detail, reservation mutation, non-mutating resolution preview, guarded apply command, stale preview rejection, and action execution readback

apps/api/src/app.ts exposes GET /api/resources/load, GET /api/resources/load/:bucketId, GET /api/resources/overloads/:overloadId, POST /api/resources/reservations, POST /api/resources/overloads/:overloadId/preview, POST /api/resources/overloads/:overloadId/apply, and GET /api/resources/audit

apps/api/src/phase6ResourcePlanningApi.test.ts covers deterministic buckets, overload severity/affected entities, dry-run no mutation, apply readback, audit/action evidence, unauthorized apply denial, tenant isolation, stale preview, and invalid preconditions
e2e/tests/phase6/*.spec.ts prove API routes through browser flow plus direct API readback, permission denial, audit/action evidence, reload persistence, and cleanup/reset

rows.requirement: Overload resolution dry-run and apply flow
evidence: apps/api/src/phase6Runtime.ts and apps/api/src/phase6ResourcePlanningApi.test.ts provide API-level dry-run/apply support for shift_work, reserve_capacity preview, permission checks, stale preview rejection, and action/audit readback

apps/web/src/ResourceLoadControlSurface.tsx exposes the recommended overload resolution flow with dry-run preview, confirmation, stale-preview recovery, refreshed resource load readback, and visible action/audit evidence
e2e/tests/phase6/overload-resolution-entry.spec.ts proves entry from the Resource Load Control surface into preview flow
e2e/tests/phase6/resolution-dry-run.spec.ts proves before/after preview does not mutate API state or audit
e2e/tests/phase6/resolution-apply-audit.spec.ts proves apply changes load, writes action/audit evidence, refreshes projection, survives reload, and reset cleans state
e2e/tests/phase6/resource-resolution-permissions.spec.ts proves read-only UI denial and direct backend denial for read-only and cross-tenant users
